actionUnits.condition: Verify MonoBehaviour_0 (m_Script guid: 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) is the Fire script attached to this GameObject and matches the provided serialized settings (BoxCollider present; Has_Rigidbody=false; timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, downMultiplier=2, onNeighborsThreshold=2, minNeighborDownDistance=3; mainEffectGo fileID=1849464941111748095). Precondition: neighbors list is populated (via FireController or manual inspector) and at least 2 neighbors are within maxNeighborDistance and currently isOn=true, with at least one neighbor positioned lower by more than minNeighborDownDistance to test the accelerated turn-on path. Expected: CheckNeighborState() sets shouldTurnOn=true; over up to timeToOn (random between 40-60s, accelerated by downMultiplier if hasDownNeighbor), Update() turns this fire on (isOn=true), resets timers, and UpdateFireState(true) plays ParticleSystem on mainEffectGo, notifies neighbors, and calls FireController.Instance.FireChanged(+1). Note: There are no UnityEvents (no m_Calls) and no public parameterless methods to invoke directly on Fire; this step relies on automatic Update() processing once preconditions are met.

Precondition: This fire is currently on (isOn=true) after the prior step. Using the water system (external to this object) that calls IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3 normal), deliver repeated hits such that each hit occurs before maxHitInterval (2s) elapses, accumulating at least totalHitTime=5s of currentHitTime. Expected: Update() detects currentHitTime >= totalHitTime within maxHitInterval windows, sets isOn=false, resets shouldTurnOn/timeToOnDelta/isTurningOff/currentHitTime, and UpdateFireState(true) stops ParticleSystem on mainEffectGo, notifies neighbors, and calls FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1).

Negative path for turning off. Precondition: Ensure this fire isOn=true again (e.g., repeat neighbor-on precondition from step 1 until it turns on). Then apply a single WaterHit (via the external water system) to start isTurningOff=true and set currentHitInterval=0. Do not apply any further hits and wait longer than maxHitInterval (2s). Expected: Update() detects currentHitInterval >= maxHitInterval and cancels the turn-off attempt (isTurningOff=false, currentHitTime reset to 0) without stopping the fire.
